Off Calendar Jan-29-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: TO BE HEARD IN DEPT. 301. JUDGE ROBERTSON RECUSES HIMSELF. (CW)

Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Complaint.
Off Calendar Jan-29-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Mary Alexander, Esq., (415-433-4440)appeared on behalf of plaintiff Harriet Kaplinsky. Mordecai D. Boone, Esq., (415-544-1900)appeared on behalf of defendant Philip Morris. Gayle L. Gough, Esq., (415-781-7900) appeared on behalf of defendant Brown & Williamson. Lorianne G. Conklin, Esq., (415-544-1900) appeared on behalf of Lorrilard Tobacco Co.

As to the 5th cause of action the demurrer was overruled and the caption is stricken. As to the 10th cause of action for punitive damages the demurrer was overruled and the caption is stricken. As to the 7th cause of action for unfair business practices the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend complaint to indicate under which capacity the plaintiff proceeds. As to the 8th cause of action for negligent false advertising the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend complaint to indicate under which capacity the plaintiff proceeds. As to the 9th cause of action for intentional false advertising the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend complaint to indicate under which capacity the plaintiff proceeds. (JJM/CW) Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855
